title: Structures of the chemical steps involved in the CO2 fixation by Rubisco
author: Oscar Douglas
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
These structures are fragments representing the active site of Rubilose-1,5-diphosphate carboxylase/oxygenase (RUBISCO) in the chemical steps leading to carbon dioxide fixation. Gready etal proposed them as a possible reaction mechanism 1  and we optimized F3 and F9 further with the B3LYP/6-31G(d,p) method due to deviations from the reported energies in the original publication. 

 All these optimized structures of the chemical steps served as input for the electronic structure calculations with the projector-embedding technique using various combinations of (post-HF) WF -in- DFT schemes reported in our publication. 

   

 
	 Kannappan, B.; Gready, J. E., J.Am.Chem.Soc .,  2008 ,130, 15063–15080
